HomePage.title: Welcome to Valora
HomePage.description: This page gives you a simple overview of what the system already does and how it supports your daily work.

status.title: System ready to use
status.description: You can already navigate the workspace, organize your structure, and evolve your records safely.

workspace.title: Current workspace
workspace.description: You are in the protected area with an active session and everything prepared for a smooth workflow.

locale.title: Language and region
locale.description: The platform is already prepared to support multiple languages without losing navigation consistency.

entryFlow.title: Current flow
entryFlow.description: At sign-in, the system already identifies your context so you can continue from the right place without manual setup.

tenant.title: Organization and team
tenant.description: Define who is part of the operation and which role each person has in daily work.

location.title: Location structure
location.description: Organize your operation by units and hierarchy levels to keep a clear view of the whole.

record.title: Organized records
record.description: Keep key business information in one place with better follow-up and continuity.

import.title: Data import
import.description: Bring external data into the system in a more practical and reliable way.

process.title: Processing
process.description: Run routines over your structured data to support analysis and decision-making.

audit.title: Audit and history
audit.description: Track changes over time to improve transparency and operational confidence.

ConfigurationPage.title: Organization, who can act, and which context
ConfigurationPage.description: Before day-to-day operations, Valora needs three things spelled out: which contracting organization this is (the tenant), who may act inside it (members and roles), and which slice of context data lives in, scopes: branches, projects, sites, whatever matches your operation. This screen is where you maintain that backbone; without it, records and permissions lack a clear frame of reference. The locations tree lives under its own sidebar entry and always follows the active scope you pick in the header.

organization.title: Tenant
organization.description: The contracting organization for this workspace: the name shown in the top bar, tenant switcher, and other surfaces. Here you align display name (what people see every day) and full legal or formal name when you need an official reference. Changes apply to the tenant active in your session; if you manage more than one, switch from the menu before editing.

member.title: Members
member.description: A member is a person tied to this tenant in the product, not just the sign-in account, but the link with role (master, administrator, member) and status. Everyone listed here can operate in this organization; roles and status control what they can change, including sensitive configuration.

scope.title: Scope
scope.description: A scope is a context unit inside the tenant: where records, and later the locations hierarchy, stay separated instead of mixed together. Think branch, job site, venue, or project,  the label is yours, but the job is always to isolate data that should not appear in one pile. The scope selected in the header is what applies when you work on locations and anything that depends on it.

ModulePlaceholderPage.description: {module} is already connected to the main navigation and uses this placeholder screen until the first functional flow is delivered.

status.title: Navigable destination available
status.description: This page is enough to validate navigation, active menu state, and incremental rollout of the module.

entry.title: Entry point already connected
entry.description: {module} now has its own URL, active sidebar item, and a clickable placeholder for flow review.

state.title: Visual behavior aligned
state.description: Hover, selected state, and the left indicator follow the same visual rules used by the other menus in the application.

next.title: Next evolution
next.description: The next delivery for this module can replace this placeholder with the first real workflow without rebuilding global navigation.

home.title: Return to the entry point
home.description: Go back to Home to review the current context and the next steps for the initial setup.
home.label: Go to Home

configuration.title: Open Configuration
configuration.description: Keep moving through the administrative area while this module's functional flows are still evolving.
configuration.label: Go to Configuration

MemberConfigurationPage.title: Members
MemberConfigurationPage.description: Edit the records of the members linked to the active tenant. Name, role, and status stay centralized in this directory.

list.empty: No members were found for the active tenant.

editor.selectToEdit: Select a member in the list to view and edit their details.

history.title: Change history
history.description: Review recent changes to member records: who changed what, when, and a clear summary of each update.

profile.displayNameHint: Used across the tenant's internal surfaces.
profile.nameHint: Use the most complete name for administrative reference.

access.title: Access
access.description: Role and status control the member's reach inside this tenant.
access.emailHint: The email for this relationship remains governed by the authenticated account or the original invite.

MemberConfigurationPage.readOnlyNotice: You can review the member directory, but only master or admin members can edit records.
MemberConfigurationPage.protectedRecordNotice: This record is protected by the current permissions. Select another member to edit.

error.save: Could not save the member. Try again.
error.delete: Could not delete the member. Try again.

ScopeConfigurationPage.title: Scopes
ScopeConfigurationPage.description: Edit the minimum structural foundation of the active tenant. Each scope defines an initial slice that will support later records and modules.
ScopeConfigurationPage.empty: No scopes were found for the active tenant.

history.title: Change history
history.description: Review recent changes to scopes for this tenant: who made each change, when, and a summary of what changed.

identity.nameHint: Use a short label to identify the scope, such as Poultry, Soy, or Milk.
identity.displayNameHint: Explain the scope slice with more context, such as Poultry for egg production.

info.title: Information
info.description: Summary of the scope in focus: values saved on the server, and what this session may change.
info.createHint: You are defining a new slice for the active tenant. Save to register it in the directory.

ScopeConfigurationPage.readOnlyNotice: You can review the scope directory, but only master or admin members can create or edit structural records.
ScopeConfigurationPage.selectPrompt: Select a scope from the list to edit the structural record.

error.save: Could not save the scope. Try again.
error.create: Could not create the scope. Try again.
error.delete: Could not delete the scope. Try again.

LocationConfigurationPage.title: Locations
LocationConfigurationPage.description: In this panel you see your location tree. The hierarchy appears on the left; beside it is the form to edit the name and other information about the location, plus the history panel. Select a location in the tree to edit it, or click the New button to create a new subdivision at that location.

list.empty: No locations were found for the selected scope.
list.emptyScope: Create a scope first before editing the location hierarchy.
list.missingCurrentScope: Set an active scope from the header selector before editing the location hierarchy.

history.title: Change history
history.description: Review recent changes to your location hierarchy: who changed what, when, and a summary of each update.

identity.nameHint: Use a short name to identify this location. For example: Willow instead of Willow Farm, Brookside instead of Brookside Poultry, or East instead of East Field.
identity.displayNameHint: Enter the full name of the location. For example: Willow Farm instead of Willow, Brookside Poultry instead of Brookside, or East Field instead of just East.

structure.title: Information
structure.description: Summary of the location in focus, with the level in the hierarchy and the order among siblings of the same location.
structure.levelHint: Counted from the scope root (1 = root).
structure.orderHintEdit: Used when listing locations at the same level.
structure.orderHintCreate: Assigned when you save the new location.

LocationConfigurationPage.dragDropHint: Drag a handle to reorder before or after a sibling, or drop on a row to move under that location.
LocationConfigurationPage.readOnlyNotice: You can review the location directory, but only master or admin members can create or edit hierarchical records.

error.load: Could not load the location directory. Try again.
error.save: Could not save the location. Try again.
error.create: Could not create the location. Try again.
error.delete: Could not delete the location. Try again.
error.move: Could not move the location. Try again.

UnityConfigurationPage.title: Productive units
UnityConfigurationPage.description: This panel shows the productive-unit tree for the scope. The hierarchy is on the left; beside it is the form to edit the name and description, plus the history panel. Select an item in the tree to edit it, or use New to add a child under the chosen item.

list.empty: No productive units were found for the selected scope.
list.emptyScope: Create a scope before editing productive units.
list.missingCurrentScope: Set an active scope from the header selector before editing productive units.

history.title: Change history
history.description: Review recent changes to productive units: who changed what, when, and a summary of each update.

identity.nameHint: Use a short label you will recognize in daily work (for example: White, Female, Core).
identity.displayNameHint: Describe what this productive unit represents in the operation with a bit more context.

structure.title: Information
structure.description: Summary of the item in focus: level in the hierarchy, order among siblings, and parent link.
structure.levelHint: Counted from the scope root (1 = root).
structure.orderHintEdit: Used when listing items at the same level.
structure.orderHintCreate: Assigned when you save the new record.

UnityConfigurationPage.dragDropHint: Drag a handle to reorder before or after a sibling, or drop on a row to move under that item.
UnityConfigurationPage.readOnlyNotice: You can review productive units, but only master or admin members can create or edit hierarchical records.

error.load: Could not load the directory. Try again.

TenantConfigurationPage.title: Tenant
TenantConfigurationPage.description: These fields identify the contracting organization in the workspace, top bar, and lists. Changes apply to the active tenant of your session.

editor.selectToEdit: Select the tenant in the list to view and edit its details.

history.title: Change history
history.description: Review recent changes to the active tenant’s details: who changed what, when, and a summary of each update.

display.title: Interface name
display.description: Short label shown across the application for the active tenant.
display.nameHint: Used in the top bar, tenant switcher, and other navigation surfaces.

legal.title: Full legal name
legal.description: Official or complete name of the contracting organization.
legal.nameHint: Use the formal name; it can match the display name when no shorter label is needed.

metadata.sectionDescription: Stable identifier for this tenant in the database.

TenantConfigurationPage.readOnlyNotice: You can view tenant data, but only master or admin members can edit it.

error.save: Could not save changes. Try again.
error.delete: Could not delete the tenant. Try again.

AuditHistory.diffUnavailable: This update could not be compared to the previous record.
AuditHistory.diffEmpty: No differences were detected compared to the previous record.
AuditHistory.deletedNote: This entry represents a deletion. The log has no row payload in this case.

error.load: Could not load history. Try again.

LoginPage.title: Authenticate to enter Valora's operational workspace.
LoginPage.description: Use your Google account to sign in to Valora. Authentication already connects the account to the relationship between account, tenant, and member.

notice.authRequired: Sign in to access the protected area of the application.
notice.signedOut: Session ended. Sign in again to continue.

workspace.title: A serious workspace from the start
workspace.description: Navigation is already prepared to grow into modules without looking like a generic admin template.

traceability.title: Foundation oriented toward traceability
traceability.description: The interface starts ready to receive history, audit, and contextual explanations in the next phases.

nextStep.title: Next useful delivery
nextStep.description: After authentication, the user lands on the home page and proceeds to the initial administrative configuration.

form.title: Sign in
form.description: At this stage, the initial product access happens through Google authentication and real organizational context.
form.helper: If there is only one active relationship, access goes directly into the application. When multiple relationships or invites exist, Valora will ask you to choose the tenant.
form.accessPolicy: On the first access without a relationship or invite, Valora automatically creates an initial context so you can complete the administrative setup.
form.googleUnavailable: Google sign-in is unavailable because the public Google client identifier has not been configured in the environment yet.
form.error: Could not complete the Google sign-in. Try again.

SelectTenantPage.title: Choose how you want to enter Valora.
SelectTenantPage.description: Your Google account has been validated. Now you only need to define the active tenant that will govern the session's operational context.
SelectTenantPage.createDescription: We did not find an active tenant or pending invite. Valora can create the first context for you so you can go straight to the initial administrative configuration.
SelectTenantPage.empty: No access option was found for this account.
SelectTenantPage.genericError: Could not complete this action right now. Try again.

State.errorDescription: There was a problem rendering the interface. You can try again without leaving the current flow.
